description: The supply depot at Dalan's Hold maintains the resources needed for extended independent operation â€' the scenario that Dalan Oathspur's three days made historical. The depot is supplied by the Tavranek resupply run that comes monthly when the road is passable and is maintained at sixty-day capacity as a standing operational requirement. The supply-keeper is a Mytrone woman with thirty years of steppe logistics experience who considers the post's historical significance motivation rather than burden; she inspects every incoming supply run personally and rejects substandard items without appeal.

name: Dalan's Hold - Southeastern Road
description: The road southeast from Dalan's Hold leads toward the Gatefall Reach, the route that the Deceiver host came from and that the scattered warbands fled back along after Dark Dawn. The road is technically maintained but psychologically charged â€' the garrison's standing orders include a specific Kin-sense monitoring protocol for this direction, and the patrol circuit that covers the first ten miles southeast is the Hold's most sensitive assignment, given to the most experienced riders. The road is currently clear according to the last patrol report, filed two days ago. The next patrol departs tomorrow at dawn.

name: Wind Road - Northern Approach
description: The northern end of the Wind Road corridor, where the steppe's prevailing wind enters the terrain channel the Giants shaped millennia ago, carries a directional pressure detectable by trained riders as a reliable navigational reference. The grass here bends south with a consistency that no local topography explains â€' it is the Wind Road's effect, the Giant-modified terrain channeling air into a corridor that Mytrone children learn to read before they learn to read words. Windstones at the horizon mark the corridor's eastern and western margins. The desert trade road passes through here, and caravans use the Wind Road's direction for orientation the way ships use current.

name: Wind Road - Central Steppe Stretch
description: The central section of the Wind Road corridor runs through the heart of the Eternal Steppe's most open terrain, the Varkhon Rise visible to the west and nothing between here and the southern crescent forests to obstruct the channeled wind. Riding with the Wind Road on a clear day â€' the wind at a consistent bearing, the grass rolling in predictable patterns, the sky enormous above â€' is the experience that Mytrone culture uses as its primary metaphor for alignment with the steppe's nature. When the Wind Road disrupts here, riders know it immediately: the grass loses its directional pattern, the air pressure changes, and something is wrong.

name: Wind Road - Southern Passage
description: The southern end of the Wind Road corridor, where the channeled air releases into the broader steppe and loses its maintained direction, is identifiable to trained riders as a subtle shift from directed to diffuse wind. The transition is gradual but real, and the Mytrone who have marked it precisely consider the release point a useful navigational landmark â€' knowing you are south of the Wind Road's maintained corridor orients you relative to Tavranek, the camps, and the battlefield with useful precision. Three Windstones are visible from this point on clear days, their amber glows a triangulation tool for experienced riders.

name: Wind Road - Eastern Margin
description: The eastern edge of the Wind Road corridor borders the territory of Orthek's Stand and Asharen Camp, and the riders who patrol this margin develop a particular familiarity with the corridor's eastern behavior â€' the way it interacts with the terrain features of the mountain foothills and how those interactions can indicate weather pattern changes before conventional observation would catch them. A cracked Windstone was reported in this sector last month, its rune-circle dark for the first time in recorded history. The patrol reports that followed describe tool-mark damage rather than natural degradation, and the investigation is ongoing.

name: Wind Road - Western Margin
description: The western edge of the Wind Road corridor is where the Varkhon Rise's terrain effect combines with the Giants' wind-channeling to create the strongest expression of the corridor's directional pressure. Riders near the Varkhon Rise who lose their orientation in poor conditions use the Wind Road's western margin as their re-orientation reference â€' the wind here is the most reliable directional indicator on the steppe, stronger and more consistent than anywhere else in the corridor. Kavarn's Drift's herd-riders work this margin daily, their Wind Road reading integrated into the routine of herd management so completely that they rarely think of it as a separate skill.

name: Wind Road - Eastern Junction
description: The point where the Wind Road corridor's eastern margin meets the patrol roads from Orthek's Stand and Asharen Camp is a navigational reference point that appears on every steppe map produced in the past century. A Windstone here marks the junction â€' smaller than the Great Windstone but well-maintained, its rune-circle reliable â€' and the convergence of patrol routes makes this a natural stop point for riders from three different camp territories who often cross paths here and exchange intelligence informally before continuing. The junction's position makes it the first point at which an eastern approach to the central steppe can be detected from the Wind Road corridor.

name: Wind Road - Western Junction
description: The western junction of the Wind Road corridor meets the patrol routes from Kavarn's Drift and Veyla Crossing, creating a convergence point used by the western camps' riders as regularly as the eastern junction is used by the eastern camps. The Varkhon Rise is visible on the western horizon from here, and in clear conditions the smoke from Kavarn's Drift's headman fire â€' maintained at a height that makes it visible for ten miles â€' is detectable. The Wind Road here carries the Varkhon Rise's influence, the Rise's terrain effect amplifying the corridor's directional pressure beyond what the Giants' terrain-shaping alone would produce.

name: Wind Road - Approach to Battlefield
description: The Wind Road corridor as it approaches the Dark Dawn Battlefield from the north shows the first signs of the battlefield's influence: the grass grows slightly less freely, the Wind Road's directional pressure has a slightly different quality â€' not disrupted, but changed, as if the corridor is working harder to maintain itself through the elemental scarring of the battlefield zone. Experienced riders notice it two miles before the battlefield's edge is visible and use the change as a warning indicator. Current patrol protocols mark this point as the transition from normal patrol to heightened-awareness patrol mode.

name: Wind Road - Central Open Steppe
description: The widest and most exposed section of the Wind Road's maintained corridor is here, the terrain flat in every direction, the sky genuinely enormous, the wind consistent at the corridor's bearing. Riding across this section in good weather is the clearest expression of the steppe's fundamental character â€' movement, openness, wind, and the horizon in every direction. Mytrone culture does not record this as a landmark because it is not one: it is the steppe itself, in its essential form, and the Mytrone understand landmarks as exceptions to that form rather than destinations within it. What is landmark here is precisely the absence of exception.

name: Wind Road - Southwest Approach
description: The southwestern approach to the Wind Road corridor from Veyla Crossing's direction provides a gentle on-ramp into the maintained wind-channel for riders approaching from the forest-steppe transition. The first entry into the Wind Road's influence â€' the moment when the diffuse mixed wind of the forest edge gives way to the corridor's directed pressure â€' is something Mytrone riders describe as 'finding the road' in the same way that a sailor describes finding the current. Veyla Crossing's riders use this entry point to calibrate their Wind Road reading after extended forest patrols where the corridor's signal is blocked.

name: Wind Road - Tavranek Approach
description: The Wind Road corridor's closest approach to Tavranek runs through the open steppe north of the city, the Great Windstone's amber glow visible on the horizon when conditions are right. The corridor here carries the combined influence of the Great Windstone and the terrain channeling, making it the most reliably readable section of the Wind Road for riders who need to navigate to Tavranek in poor visibility. Far Rider patrols returning to the city use this approach as their terminal navigation reference, and the worn path along the corridor's edge shows the accumulated boot-traffic of three hundred years of riders coming home.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Western Edge
description: The western edge of the Dark Dawn Battlefield is where the battle reached its furthest extent, the evidence of the conflict visible in the dark patches of grass â€' thin, reluctant growth over scorched earth that has never fully recovered â€' and the occasional glass-fused crater where alien magic struck the steppe with enough force to fuse the soil. The smell of old burning rises from the ground on warm days, and Kin-sense begins its unpredictable flickering here: not gone, but unreliable, shifting between normal sensitivity and jarring suppression without warning. The horses of experienced riders shy here in ways that their riders do not understand and cannot prevent.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Northern Perimeter
description: The northern edge of the battlefield is where the Brotherhood of the Steppe's charge lines began, the grass here worn to near-nothing by the training exercises and memorial processions that have maintained the ground's usage without allowing it to recover. The burn-scars here are lighter than deeper in the battlefield â€' the battle moved southward during the night â€' but the Kin-sense disruption is present from the moment you cross the northern perimeter. Riders entering here feel the Will DC 16 pressure that the battlefield generates, and those who have not experienced it before are typically not warned adequately by description.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Central Zone
description: The center of the Dark Dawn Battlefield covers the area where the battle's intensity was greatest, the burn-scars dense and the glass-fused craters large. The alien-magic impact points are visible as smooth, slightly reflective depressions where the normal soil texture has been replaced by fused mineral glass. Items from the battle â€' metal fragments, the occasional Deceiver equipment piece, the rare remnant of Brotherhood arms â€' are half-buried in the glass-studded soil, and the temptation to collect them is understandable and ill-advised: the Mytrone's prohibition on removing battlefield material is not merely ceremonial. The Kin-sense disruption in this zone is severe, and disorientation follows quickly for those who do not anchor themselves.

effects.message: Your Kin-sense flickers dangerously. The central dead zone of Dark Dawn still pulses.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Eastern Flank
description: The eastern flank of the battlefield is where the Wind-Rider Pasua archers operated during the battle's main engagement, their hit-and-retreat tactics leaving a different pattern of evidence than the lancers' direct charges: widely spaced impact clusters and the occasional burned circle where a Wind-Rider unit paused and received Deceiver fire before moving again. The burn-scars here are more scattered, the battlefield archaeology reflecting movement rather than position. Sand Wraiths have been spotted on the eastern flank more often than on other sections, their presence suggesting that the battle's casualties were heaviest here in the final phase.

effects.message: Your Kin-sense is unstable here. Dark Dawn's echo has not faded.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Southern Zone
description: The southern zone of the battlefield is where the Deceiver encampment stood and where the battle ended. The burn-scars here are absolute â€' grass does not grow in the central section, has not grown since the battle, and soil samples sent to Tidebloom scholars have never produced any explanation consistent with natural fire. The glass-fused craters in this section are the largest on the battlefield, some ten feet across and six inches deep, their surfaces still carrying the faint residue of alien magic that defies identification. The Gilded Company fought here, and fragments of their non-Orekaan equipment occasionally emerge from the soil during heavy rains.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Warg Kill Zone
description: The section of the battlefield where the Deceiver war-wargs with flaming-pitch ropes caused the greatest chaos is identifiable by the burn-pattern: long, curved scorch lines in the grass where the trailing pitch ropes swept the steppe, crossing and recrossing in the chaos of the battle's initial phase. The smell of old pitch rises here on hot days, and the grass that grows in the surrounding areas avoids these lines as if it remembers. The Mytrone who study the battlefield for historical purposes find this section the hardest to document accurately â€' the burn patterns record movement too complex for straightforward interpretation.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Wind-Rider Memorial
description: A natural rise in the battlefield's eastern section has been maintained as a Wind-Rider memorial â€' a position that oral tradition says was where the Pasua Wind-Riders made their final charge, the one that shattered the last organized Deceiver resistance. The rise is the only point in the battlefield where any Mytrone-sanctioned construction has been placed: a single stone marker, uninscribed, which the Pasua community requested be left without text because the act of reading words is a slower form of communication than the act of feeling the wind that passes through them. Kin-sense at this marker registers a warmth rather than the disruption normal to the battlefield.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Gilded Company Stand
description: The position where the Gilded Company made their stand after their defection to the Brotherhood is documented in their own records, preserved by their descendants the Farborn in Titan's Rest. The ground here shows the evidence of a disciplined defensive position â€' the alien-equipment fragments here are from the Company's own arms, not Deceiver weapons, and the pattern of burn-scars suggests a perimeter defense that held for several hours before the battle's end. The Mytrone maintain no specific memorial here, unsure of the protocol for honoring fighters from another plane who died for Oreka's defense, but they do not disturb the ground.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Dead Zone Center
description: The absolute center of the battlefield's Dead Zone is where the Deceiver command camp stood and where the encampment's alien anchoring magic was strongest. Kin-sense goes completely silent here â€' not the flickering disruption of the battlefield's edge but a total absence, the Void sensation that triggers the deepest instinctual alarm in every Kin practitioner. Glass-fused craters cluster in this section, the largest four feet deep, and the alien equipment fragments here are more numerous and better preserved than elsewhere. Flamewarg cult activity has been reported near this section's perimeter, suggesting that someone knows what to look for in the debris. Nothing biological grows in the central thirty yards.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Southern Exit Road
description: The road south from the Dark Dawn Battlefield leads toward the Burnt Hollows â€' the network of collapsed Deceiver burrow-forts beneath the steppe south of the battlefield â€' and eventually toward Sylrek Camp. The road is marked with the double-slash warning sign used throughout the steppe for significant danger zones, and the patrol markers here are the most frequently updated on the steppe, reflecting the active intelligence situation on the battlefield's southern margin. The new tunnel entrances reported by scouts three weeks ago are visible from this road as disturbed earth mounds at irregular intervals, their fresh timber reinforcement incongruous against the old burned grass.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Eastern Exit
description: The eastern edge of the battlefield, where the Wind-Rider eastern flank operations ended and the post-battle pursuit of fleeing Deceiver warbands began. The grass here transitions back to normal steppe growth within a hundred yards, the battlefield's influence ending with the abruptness that characterizes all its borders â€' as if a line was drawn rather than the burn-damage fading gradually. Far Rider patrol markers on the eastern boundary are current, the patrols covering this edge more frequently than any other battlefield section due to the intelligence situation in the southeastern steppe.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Brotherhood Advance Line
description: The advance line of the Brotherhood's initial engagement â€' the position from which the Far Rider lancers made their first charge â€' is marked by a subtle elongated depression in the grass where generations of memorial-riders have retraced the original charge line. The depression is shallow enough to be invisible unless you know to look, and most visitors miss it entirely. Those who know steppe archaeology read it immediately: a formation line seven hundred yards across, compressed into the soil by the weight of hundreds of horses standing in formation before the charge. The wind across this line always moves in the same direction as the original charge, an effect that no one has explained.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Scattered Debris Zone
description: A section of the battlefield where the chaos of the battle's mid-phase is most evident in the random scatter of equipment fragments, burn-scars, and glass craters with no evident tactical pattern. Historians who have attempted to reconstruct the battle from this section's physical evidence have concluded that the mid-phase was genuinely chaotic â€' neither side in organized formation, the fighting fragmenting into dozens of simultaneous smaller engagements across several square miles of burning steppe. The debris here includes items from both sides mixed together, the clearest physical evidence of how close the outcome was during the hours before dawn.

name: Dark Dawn Battlefield - Hawk Rider Position
description: A position at the battlefield's northern edge that oral tradition identifies as where the Brotherhood's hawk companions â€' used for real-time battle communication across the chaotic engagement â€' were stationed with their handlers. The hawks flew message-carrying loops between unit commanders throughout the battle, their presence noted in multiple independent accounts. The ground here shows evidence of a static position â€' packed earth consistent with a standing camp rather than a charge line â€' and a small shelter-remnant of cut timber that has been preserved by the same mysterious process that maintains other organic remnants on the battlefield far beyond their natural lifespan.

name: Burnt Hollows - Entrance
description: The Burnt Hollows are the network of collapsed Deceiver burrow-forts beneath the steppe south of the Dark Dawn Battlefield, partially mapped by Far Rider scouts and extending deeper than anyone has followed. The entrance here â€' one of seven known surface access points â€' descends into a timber-reinforced tunnel that smells of old earth, stale air, and the faint chemical residue of Deceiver construction materials that three centuries have not fully dispersed. The new tunnel entrances reported three weeks ago are visible from this position: fresh timber reinforcement, recently disturbed soil, and footprints in the loose earth that are smaller than any Flamewarg cult member's foot. Someone â€' something â€' is digging from below.

name: Varkhon Rise - Southern Approach
description: The southern approach to the Varkhon Rise â€' the series of long, low hills running north-south through the steppe's western quarter â€' begins here where the flat central steppe begins its gentle rise toward the Ridge's first hills. The transition is subtle, the terrain change barely perceptible in the first mile, but the wind changes immediately: the Wind Road's channeled pressure becomes more directional, the air carrying the slightly different scent of the higher ground. Kavarn's Drift's herd-riders work this approach daily, and their passage has compressed the approach track into something that functions as a road without being maintained as one.

name: Varkhon Rise - First Hill
description: The first significant elevation of the Varkhon Rise provides a view south across the central steppe that is Kavarn's Drift's primary tactical asset: two miles of open ground visible from the hilltop, with the Wind Road's corridor detectable by the grass-bending pattern for another three beyond that. The hill's leeward side â€' the eastern slope, sheltered from the prevailing wind â€' is where the camp pitches in its winter position, the shelter reducing the cold-season wind-chill to manageable levels. Windstones are visible on the hilltops to the north, their positions originally chosen by the Giants to maximize their wind-stabilizing effect.

name: Varkhon Rise - Central Ridge
description: The central section of the Varkhon Rise is the highest point of the north-south hill chain, its ridgeline providing the longest sight-lines available anywhere on the Eternal Steppe. On clear days both the Giant's Teeth to the north and the crescent forests of the southern margin are visible simultaneously â€' the full north-south extent of the steppe readable from a single position. The Mytrone use this ridge for Wind Gathering communications by mirror-signal, and the position is maintained by a semi-permanent watch station whose current focus is the cracked Windstone report in the corridor to the east.

name: Varkhon Rise - Northern Summit
description: The northernmost significant summit of the Varkhon Rise looks toward the desert approaches and the Giant's Teeth beyond. The wind here is coldest â€' the Giant's Teeth's snowfields affect the temperature gradient at this latitude â€' and the Windstones on this summit glow most reliably, their amber light consistent through storms that extinguish fires and reduce visibility to feet. Headman Greywind comes to this summit annually before the Wind Gathering, sitting here in the wind for an unspecified period before descending to lead the procession. He has not explained the practice, and no one has asked.

name: Varkhon Rise - Rider's Teeth
description: The ring of twelve standing stones on the Varkhon Rise's highest point is distinct from the Windstones â€' their rune-circles are oriented toward air rather than earth, and they hum audibly when the wind is strong. Mytrone legend says the Giants used this site to speak with the Wind Lord directly, and the legend has not been disproven by any investigation. The stones are six to eight feet tall, paler than the Windstones, and arranged in a precise circle fifty feet across. Air magic cast within the circle is amplified beyond the Windstones' effect â€' gust of wind spells require Fort DC 16 to resist near the stones, and spontaneous air-elemental manifestations have been reported during high-wind conditions.

name: Varkhon Rise - Western Slope
description: The western slope of the Varkhon Rise descends toward the steppe's edge and the distant connection to the Deepwater Marches. The terrain here is rougher than the eastern slopes, the wind exposure higher, and the vegetation more sparse â€' the plants that grow on the windward western face have adapted to conditions that would discourage most steppe species. The view west is of open grassland that grades gradually into the transitional terrain before the Deepwater Marches' edge, a distance of many days' ride that is nonetheless visible in principle from the Rise's western ridgeline on clear days.

name: Varkhon Rise - Kavarn's Drift Northern Pastures
description: The northern pastures of Kavarn's Drift's territory extend along the Varkhon Rise's eastern slopes, the thick grass of the Rise's leeward areas supporting the large-frame breeding stock that is the camp's primary commercial asset. The pastures here are better-watered than the central steppe, the Rise's elevation capturing precipitation and the Windstone influence maintaining the soil moisture that produces the grass density. Herd-riders working these pastures operate at the edge of the camp's standard patrol circuit, and their daily reports on grass condition are one of the primary inputs into the annual Wind Gathering's grazing rotation discussion.

name: Varkhon Rise - Southern Pass
description: A natural pass through the Varkhon Rise's southern section allows passage between the western slopes and the open central steppe without climbing the full ridge. The pass is used by Veyla Crossing's riders on their patrol circuits and by the Halfling caravan-traders who prefer the Rise's shelter to the open steppe for the western portion of their circuit. The pass is narrow enough that a small force could hold it against a larger one â€' a tactical detail that Orthek's Stand's garrison assessment included in its most recent threat-scenario document, noting it as a potential chokepoint that nobody currently controls or watches.

name: Varkhon Rise - Windstone Cluster
description: Three Windstones in close proximity near the Varkhon Rise's midpoint create a weather-stabilization effect more powerful than any single stone, their combined rune-circle resonance producing the green-and-thick grass circle that Kavarn's Drift's camp has incorporated into its standard position. The cluster's combined glow on clear nights is visible from Tavranek's watchtower, providing a navigational reference point for night-riding between the two locations. The central stone of the three is the tallest Windstone outside Tavranek â€' fourteen feet, well-maintained, its rune-circle the most elaborately inscribed of the cluster. Riders who camp near it consistently report better rest than elsewhere on the steppe.

name: Varkhon Rise - Northern Approach Road
description: The road north from the Varkhon Rise toward Teshen Halt and the desert approaches follows the Rise's eastern base, the hills providing shelter from the prevailing westerly that makes the open steppe harder riding at this latitude. The road is the most comfortable approach to the desert border in poor weather, and caravan-traders from the desert consistently prefer it in autumn when the pre-winter winds intensify. The Windstones along this section provide navigational landmarks in conditions when the open steppe provides none, and the amber glow that marks each stone at night creates the closest thing to a road-lit highway that the Eternal Steppe offers.

name: Windstone - Central Steppe Site
description: One of the forty known Windstones standing in the open central steppe, this stone rises seven feet from the surrounding grassland, its quartz veining clear and its rune-circle in excellent condition. The grass around it grows in the characteristic thicker-and-greener circle that marks Giant-era stone influence, and the circle's edge is visible even from a distance as a slight color shift in the steppe. Far Rider patrols use this stone as a checkpoint and waypoint; the patrol log attached to its base records every patrol that has passed since the log was established six years ago. The stone has shown no anomalies in that period.

effects.message: Your Kin-sense sharpens dramatically. The Windstone's rune-circle amplifies every resonance.

name: Windstone - Eastern Cluster Site
description: Two Windstones standing within fifty feet of each other create a minor cluster in the eastern steppe, their combined rune-circle influence affecting weather across a wider radius than a single stone. The grass circle here is noticeably larger than at single-stone sites, and the cluster is a reliable navigational landmark for riders in the eastern sector. The Far Rider patrol that covers this sector has recently reported an anomaly: one of the two stones shows the beginning of a crack along its eastern face, the rune-circles near the crack showing reduced glow intensity. The inspection report is en route to Tavranek's administrative center.

effects.message: Your Kin-sense rings clear as a bell. Giant-era magic amplifies your perception.

name: Windstone - Southern Steppe Site
description: A Windstone at the southern steppe's edge, near the transition to the crescent forests, marks the Mytrone's southernmost permanent presence point. The stone is smaller than those in the central steppe â€' six feet, partially buried by soil creep â€' but its rune-circle is active and its effect on the local weather reliable. Headman Greenrein of Veyla Crossing maintains this stone as part of the Windstone maintenance work the camp has been assigned, and the most recent renewal ceremony was three months ago. The stone's position at the forest-steppe boundary means it is the one most exposed to the natural elemental interference that the forest generates.

name: Windstone - Teshkar Vale Ring Stone
description: One of four Windstones arranged in a loose ring around Teshkar Vale, this stone marks the northern approach to the Vale's sacred ground. The stones in this ring are maintained to the highest standard outside Tavranek, their rune-circles renewed at every Wind Gathering and their bases cleared of accumulated grass and soil each spring. The stone here faces the Wind Road's approach from the north, and its glow on Wind Gathering nights â€' when all four ring stones are active simultaneously â€' is said to be visible to incoming Headmen as the first sign that the Vale is in sight, before the Vale itself becomes visible over the steppe's gentle rise.

name: Windstone - Battle Perimeter Stone
description: A Windstone near the Dark Dawn Battlefield's northern perimeter has maintained its function despite proximity to the battlefield's Kin-sense disruption zone â€' a fact that Mytrone scholars find significant and that practical patrol riders simply appreciate, since it provides a reliable navigational anchor in the otherwise disorienting battlefield approach. The stone's rune-circle shows the strain of operating near the dead zone in small ways visible only to trained observers: the amber glow is slightly more orange than standard, and the grass circle's edge is irregular rather than smooth. The stone is inspected monthly and flagged for close observation.

name: Crescent Forest - Northern Shieldwood Margin
description: The northern margin of the Shieldwood crescent forest where it meets the open steppe is the most accessible section of the forest for riders, its canopy lower than the interior and the undergrowth open enough for horses. Pasua Elf communities maintain a presence here, their forest-floor camps visible as organized clearings with the living-architecture structures that Pasua building uses. The forest smell â€' humid, rich with growing things, a sharp contrast to the steppe's dry grass scent â€' reaches riders at the forest edge, and the temperature drops several degrees within the first hundred yards of canopy cover.

name: Crescent Forest - Shieldwood Interior
description: The interior of the Shieldwood crescent forest is the densest and most ecologically complex section of the steppe's edge forestry. Giant-era planting designed this forest to anchor soil and break wind, and after millennia of growth the design has evolved into something far more elaborate â€' a multi-layered ecosystem with Pasua communities at the canopy level and a forest floor that supports species found nowhere else on the steppe. The forest here is genuinely concealing: visibility drops to thirty feet in most sections, and a full warband could shelter here without being visible from the steppe margin. The Flamewarg cult has used it for exactly this purpose.

name: Crescent Forest - Southern Shieldwood
description: The southern section of the Shieldwood forest is the least-visited by Mytrone riders, its canopy dense and its approaches from the Deepwater Marches border direction making it a natural staging area for threats moving northwest. Pasua forest-wardens cover this section, their knowledge of the forest interior providing the intelligence that Far Rider scouts cannot generate in terrain this complex. Current reports from the southern Shieldwood section show increased Flamewarg cult activity in the past month â€' movement patterns suggesting preparation for something rather than the usual low-level presence. The report is with Tavranek's Far Rider command, flagged priority.

name: Steppe - Eastern Transition Zone
description: The eastern transition between the Eternal Steppe and the Gatefall Reach's forested hills is a region of mixed terrain â€' grassland interrupted by rocky outcrops, isolated tree-stands, and the beginning of the soil change that marks the shift from steppe to hill-country. Flamewarg cult remnants use the terrain complexity here as operational cover, and Far Rider patrols in this zone operate with the heightened attention that mixed terrain requires. The Silence Breach's historical location is accessible from this direction, and the awareness of that proximity shapes patrol behavior in the eastern transition zone in ways that are both operationally appropriate and psychologically taxing.

name: Steppe - Northwestern Margin
description: The northwestern corner of the Eternal Steppe where the grassland approaches the Deepwater Marches' edge is the least-traveled section of the steppe's perimeter, its distance from the major camps and the complex terrain of the Marches border making it a low-priority patrol area. The grass here grows into the transitional vegetation that precedes the Marches' jungle character, and the wind off the Apelian Sea â€' detectable at this latitude as a moister, saltier quality in the air â€' adds a coastal character to what is otherwise steppe landscape. Halfling caravan-traders who work the western circuit sometimes camp here between runs, finding the margin's solitude useful for accounting purposes.
